textPlain: I have been trying to figure out how this works in concert with Git (or SCM in general). Is one of the developers in the session merely responsible for it?

Zed is lovely and I hope it becomes super successful but this kind of mass collaboration might be ok for meeting minutes... maybe. But thinking of it for coding it gives me shingles. Code by mass committee. Yikes.

I was very surprised to find a "forum" integrated in Zed when I first opened it. But to be honest, it is not something I ever felt the need for and overall I don't like having this in my text editor. So far it never got in my way and that's a good thing, I hope it stays that way :)
This looks more like a collab note-taking app. Don't know about code since i don't code anymore inside an editor but for collab things who knows
I hadn't realized Zed was built from the ground up to support collaborative programming. I liked it already, and I like it even more now.

Haha, yeah. So true.Anyways, any news on the ability to see history threads for Claude/Codex instances? Huge weakness vs. VSCode implementation and clearly blocking given that many developers have moved over to writing 90-100% of their code using these services.
